title: Phase 4.5: In-PWA upgrade button on /admin
summary: Once /admin (Phase 4) and bin/deploy.sh (Phase 1.5) both exist, surface a Deploy panel on /admin that shows current vs available commit and runs the deploy script with live log tail — no SSH required for routine updates.
outOfScope: Rolling back to a previous commit from the UI (manual git for now)
Choosing a non-main branch or a specific sha to deploy (always deploys origin/main)
Push-based auto-deploy on commit (Phase 1.5 explicitly defers GH Actions)
technicalConsiderations: Depends on Phase 1.5 (bin/deploy.sh exists and is invocable from the pwa container) and Phase 4 (PWA /admin route + admin auth + the persistent observability store P4 settled on)
Security: this endpoint is RCE-equivalent — admin auth must be a separate scope from the regular chat cookie, and the endpoint must be inaccessible without it

If the pwa container is itself restarted by the deploy, the in-flight HTTP response will drop — the SSE consumer should reconnect and resume from the run record, and the run lock must clear on container start (any 'running' rows older than the deploy timeout get marked 'failed' on boot)

Deploy invocation path: pwa container has the host repo bind-mounted at /opt/brainbot read-only and uses docker socket access to run compose commands — OR pwa shells out to a small host-side helper via SSH-to-localhost. Pick the docker-socket-mount path; document the security implication (anyone who pops the pwa container has root-equivalent on the host)
Deploy run records reuse whatever observability store /admin uses (introduced in Phase 4); no new store is added for this feature
Risks: deploy-during-deploy livelock if pwa restarts itself mid-run (mitigated by the boot-time lock cleanup), log retention growth (rotate after 90 days)

references.name: Coolify
references.why: Open-source self-hosted PaaS with the same in-UI deploy pattern: git-pull-and-rebuild button, live SSE log tail, deploy history with success/failure status, per-resource rollback. Worth reading their deploy worker + log streaming implementation before building ours.
